items.statement: The project should keep AI useful and bounded rather than making every interaction AI-driven.
The proposed wall would damage Big Bend tourism and could hollow out nearby towns such as Terlingua.
Chuck and Susan have already contacted multiple representatives and agencies about the wall issue.
If wall plans proceed, scenic access, wildlife movement, and tourism economics could all degrade at once.
The family plans to attend an Austin protest related to the Big Bend wall issue.
The Burro land is remote enough that visits are effectively full-day or multi-day logistics, not casual stop-ins.
Road improvements may increase traffic and outside interest in nearby parcels, reducing privacy and control.
Chuck and Susan want to explore buying the adjoining parcel to prevent unwanted development next to their land.
An appraisal-district mistake caused taxes to be paid on the wrong parcel for years.
The current parcel remains unsellable until heirs release the lien and probate is resolved.
Will the heirs actively resolve the lien release and probate path, or will the parcel remain in limbo longer than planned?
A skid steer or small backhoe is viewed as the practical equipment required for the water project.
Should the water-project equipment plan assume purchase, resale, community rental, or a hybrid model?
Future language help should prioritize usable Spanish support tied to practical operator needs.
